title: Router v3 Classifier Output
description: Frozen wire format for the v3 Pi prompt router classifier. One JSON object per classify.py invocation, serialized as a single line and terminated with a trailing newline. Consumed by pi/extensions/prompt-router.ts at the TypeScript boundary.

schema_version.description: Semver of this output contract. TypeScript router compares against a known-good set and falls back gracefully on mismatch.

primary.description: The cheapest acceptable route the classifier predicts for this prompt. The router dispatches the first attempt to this route.

candidates.description: Candidate routes with calibrated marginal confidence. Ordered by ascending ordinal cost (model_tier first, then effort). MUST include the primary route. Used by the runtime for uncertainty fallback and by /router-explain for observability.

confidence.description: Calibrated probability that primary is the cheapest acceptable route for this prompt (top-1 calibrated confidence). Consumers may apply an UNCERTAIN_THRESHOLD below which the router biases toward a costlier candidate.

reason.description: Short human-readable explanation of the route choice. Observability only; MUST NOT drive dispatch.

ambiguity_flag.description: Echo of the predicted ambiguity level, mirroring the corpus ambiguity field. Observability only; runtime uncertainty handling is driven by confidence, not this flag.

predicted_domain.description: Classifier's best-guess domain tag. Observability only; the runtime does not dispatch on this.

model_version.description: SHA of the classifier artifact that produced this output. Required in production; optional in contract-schema terms because offline tools may omit it.

ensemble_rule.description: For ensemble/confgate classifiers: which combination rule fired (lgb-confident | agree | t2-overrides | lgb-fallback | veto). Observability only; MUST NOT drive dispatch.

model_tier.description: Ordinal model tier. Ordering: Haiku < Sonnet < Opus.

effort.description: Ordinal reasoning effort tier. Ordering: none < low < medium < high.

confidence.description: Calibrated marginal probability that this candidate route is acceptable for the prompt.
